t
Dear Ms- Ayers-Johnson:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the 'Act').1 Si.r"" you do not have a
specific de,cision pending before you nolv, we are keating this requlst as one for informal
assrstance.'
QUf,STTON
Farn PolrrrcAI- PRACTTcES CouurssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 9SBl4-2329
(916) 322-56@ o Fax (916) 322-0986
January 24,2005
Darlene Ayers-Johnson, Commissioner
Port of Oakland
c/o Friends of Faith, Inc.
11001 Lochard Street
Oakland, CA94605
Re: Your Request for fnformal Assictance
Our File No. l-04474
As a member of the Board of Port Commissioners in Oakland, are you precluded
from participating in decisions affecting individuals and businesses that -uk" 
'
contributions to Friends of Faith, Inc., a 5Ol(c)(3) organization, from which you receive
consultant fees as its executive director?
CONCLUSION
No. Since your income is from anonprofit entiqr, you are not disqualifid from
participating in decisions having a material financial etrect on any individuals or
businesses that make charitable contributions to Friend ofFaith, inc.
FACTS
You are an appointed official withthe Board ofPort Commissioners in Oakland
(the "port commission'). As a port commissionetr, you receive no compensation from the
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2,sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2lnformal Assistance does not p.oiid" the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83114; regulation lg3r9(cx3), copy enclosed.) 
-


FileNo. I-04-274
Page No. 2
port or the City of Oakland. Port commissioners are appointed by the mayor of Oakland
and confirmed by the Oakland City Council. The term of the appointnent is four years.
You me also the executive director ofFriends of Faith, lnc., anonprofit breast
cancer organization formed under section 501(cX3) of the Internal Revenue Code. You
operate as an independent contractor or consultant, and not as an employee ofFriends of
Faith, Inc. You are compensated through a signed contract and consultant fees.
Additionally, you are not a voting bomd member of the board, nor do you have decision-
making responsibility for policy as it is decided by the executive committee of the board.
You are asking if it would be a conflict of interest for you to vote on items
affecting individuals or businesses that make contributions to Friends of Faitll Inc.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 of the Act prohibits a public official from makingpartrcrpating in
making or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental
decision in which the official has a financial interest. In order to determine whether the
prohibition in section 87100 applies to a given decision, regulation l8T0Oprovides an
eight-step analysis, as discussd below- The general rule, however, is that a conflict of
interest exists when a public official makes or participates in a governmental decision
which may have a reasonably foreseeable and material financial effect on one or more of
his or her financial interests.
Steps I and 2 - Is the individual a public official and will he/she be making
participating in making using or attempting or use his/her oflicial;rosition to
influence a governmental decision?
As a member of the Board of Port Commissioners, you are a public official as
defined in the Act. (Section 82048-) A public official "makes a govemmental decision"
when he or she votes on a mattetr, appoints a p€rson, obligates or comrnits his or her
agency to any course of action or enters into a contractual agreemenl @egulation
l87Wl.) You indicated that you would be voting on matters that come before the port
commission.
Step 3 - Identi$ the official's economic interests.
Under section 87103 ofthe Act, there are six different types ofecommic interests
which may result in a conflict of interest for a public official. The interests identified in
your advice rquest only pertain to your source of income.
Source of Income: A public official has an economic interest in any source of
income, including promised income, which aggregates to $500 or more within 12 months
priorto the time a decision is rendered. (Section 87103(c); regulation 18703.3.)
You receive income as the executive director of Friends of Faith,Inc. While you
did not specifically indicate the value of that income, we will assume it aggregates to


FileNo. I-04-274
Page No. 3
$500 or more during a given l2-month period- For conflict-of-interest pulposes, we
would not pierce through to persons who make charitable contributions to a nonprofit
entity. (Fazio Advice Letter, No. 4-93442.) We thus only need to analyzethe effect of
your decisions on your source of income, Friends ofFaith, Inc-
Step 4 - Is the economic interest directly or indirectly involved in a govemmental
decision?
You do not have a specific decision pending before you now to analyzethis step
to a specific set of facts. However, Friends of Faitla Inc. would be "directly''involvedln
a govemmental decision if it:
'(1) Initiates the proceeding in which the decision will be made by
filing an applicatiorg claim, appeal, or similarrequest or;
"(2) Is a named party in, or is the subject o{, the proceeding
concerning the decision before the official or the official,s agency. A
person is the subject of a proceeding if a decision involves the issuancg
renewal, approval, denial or revocation of any license, permit, or other
entitlement to, or contract witlr, the subject p€trson.,, @egulation
18704.1.)
If Friends of Faith, Inc. were the applicant or the subject of the decision before the
port commission, Friends of Faith, Inc. would b directly involved. However, if an
individual or business that is a contributor to Friends of Fait[ Inc. comes before the port
commission, Friends ofFaith would be indirectly involved.
Step 5 - What is the applicable materiality standard?
Indtrectly Affected
In order to determine if an indirect economic interest is material, the Commission
has created a set of standards for nonprofit organizations- (Regulation 18705.3.) For
example, in the case of a relatively small nonprofit entity, where the gross annual receipts
are $100,000 or less, the effect of a decision will be material if:
'(i) The decision will result in an increase or decrease of the entiql's gross armual
receipts for a fiscal year in the amount of$l0,0oo ormore.
(ii) The decision will cause the entity to incur or avoid additional expenses or to
reduce or eliminate existing expenses for a fiscal year in the arnount of S2,500 or
more.
(iii) The decision will result in an increase or decrease in the value of the entity's
assets or liabilities in the amount of $10,000 or more-,, (Regulation
18705.3(b)(2XF).)


File No. I-04-274
Page No. 4
Thus, if an individual or business comes before the port commission and it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision would afilect Friends ofFaith,Inc-, the effect will be
considered material if it meets the thresholds in regulation 18705.3 set out above.
Step 6 - Is it substantially likely the governmental decision will meet the materiality
standards?
A material financial effect is "reasonably foreseeable" when it is "substantially
likely''to occur- This means that one or more of the materiality standards applicable io
that economic interest will be met as a result ofthe governmental decision. -Wh"r,
Friends of Faith, Inc. is the applicant before the port commission, it is reasonably
foreseeable that the materiality standards will be met.
If an individual or business that is a contributor to Friemds of Faith, Inc. comes
before the port commissiorl you will have to determine whether it will be substantially
likely that the decision will have a material financial effect on Friends of Fait[ Inc., in
which case you would be required to disqualify yourself from the governmental decision.
This should be done on a case-by-case basis each time a contributor to Friends of Faith,
Inc. comes before the port commission.
steps 7 andl S - The *public generally' and *legally required participation.
exceptions.
Since you do not have a decision pending before you now, we cannot analyze
these two exceptions to a specific fact pattenr_
I trust this answers your question. Should you have a specific decision pending
before you and need firrther assistance, or have any other questions regarding this matler,
please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General
t/a
J,,
Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosure
JETjg

sections.conclusion:
No. Since your income is from anonprofit entiqr, you are not disqualifid from
participating in decisions having a material financial etrect on any individuals or
businesses that make charitable contributions to Friend ofFaith, inc.
